mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 5.0% fall below the 20-day moving average for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals expanding consumer demand and construction activity, creating positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with both GDPC1 and HOUST in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 12-month rise in CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank rate hikes, creating headwinds for equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns exceed 1.5% in 80% of periods where CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals weakening manufacturing activity, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in INDPRO < 100 periods exceed the 20-day moving average for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k signals strong labor market dynamics that drive corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in PAYEMS > 150k periods fall below the 50-day moving average for 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in consumer credit (RSAFS) below 440 billion signals reduced household spending capacity, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in RSAFS < 440B periods exceed 2.0% in 75% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105 signals robust manufacturing growth that drives equity earnings optimism, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO > 105 periods fall below the 20-day moving average for 4 consecutive months.
